title: Tic Tac Toe
summary: Design a modular Tic Tac Toe game supporting two players on an N x N board with dynamic win-checking.
requirements: Support customizable board sizes (default 3x3).
Alternating turn sequence for Player X and Player O.
Check win condition in O(1) time after each move.
Detect draw states when all cells are filled without a winner.
erDiagram:
GameEngine [1] <---> [1] Board
GameEngine [1] <---> [2] Player
Board [1] <---> [N*N] Cell
Cell [1] <---> [1] Symbol
classes: GameEngine (Runs turn iterations and checks states)
approach: To check wins in O(1) time, keep rows and cols count arrays for each player symbol, along with main and anti-diagonal counters.

title: Traffic Signal
summary: Design a Traffic Signal Controller that transitions between Red, Yellow, and Green states with dynamic durations.
requirements: Maintain active signal light status (Red, Yellow, Green).
Process state transitions sequentially: Red -> Green -> Yellow -> Red.
Allow dynamic duration override configurations for active states.
erDiagram:
TrafficSignalController [1] <---> [*] TrafficLight
TrafficLight [1] <---> [1] SignalState
approach: Use the State pattern to prevent massive switch-case statements. Each state holds its display rules and references the next state in sequence.

title: Chess Clock
summary: Design a two-player Chess Clock tracking match timing, dynamic increments (Fischer delay), and flags.
requirements: Dual timers tracking Player 1 and Player 2 remaining time.
Switch active clock cleanly on player turn switch.
Support time increment increments (adds N seconds after move).
Trigger notifications if a player's timer runs out (Flagged).
erDiagram:
ChessClock [1] <---> [2] PlayerTimer
ChessClock [1] <---> [1] ClockState
approach: Implement thread-safe pause/resume triggers. Protect state mutations from multiple button presses during intense time scrambles.

title: Logger
summary: Design a log framework supporting info, debug, and error levels directing messages to console, files, or database targets.
requirements: Levels: INFO, DEBUG, ERROR.
Each logger in chain handles messages based on threshold levels.
Support multiple sinks: File, Console, and Database.
erDiagram:
Logger [1] <---> [0..1] Logger (Next Link)
Logger [1] <---> [1..*] LogSink
approach: Implement the Chain of Responsibility pattern. Link standard log handlers (Info -> Debug -> Error) so request propagation is automatic.

title: Coffee Machine
summary: Design a Coffee Machine controller that selects recipes, validates ingredients inventory, accepts payments, and dispenses coffee.
requirements: Support multiple recipes (Espresso, Latte, Cappuccino).
Check ingredients levels (water, milk, beans) before starting brew.
Lock machine during dispensing and update inventory levels.
erDiagram:
CoffeeMachine [1] <---> [1] Inventory
CoffeeMachine [1] <---> [*] Recipe
Inventory [1] <---> [*] Ingredient
classes: Recipe (Coffee beans weight, milk vol, water vol, cost)
approach: Represent structural recipes as immutable objects. Protect updates to the shared ingredients map using synchronized blocks or concurrent maps.

title: Vending Machine
summary: Design a Vending Machine that transitions cleanly between states: Idle, Waiting for Coins, Dispensing, Out of Stock.
requirements: Display items with prices and quantities.
Accept coins/cash inputs, calculate balance, and return change.
Ensure transaction safety: don't release items unless funds are confirmed.
erDiagram:
VendingMachine [1] <---> [1] State
VendingMachine [1] <---> [1] Inventory
Inventory [1] <---> [*] Product
classes: State (Interface with selectProduct, insertCoin, pressButton, dispense)
approach: Use the State pattern to represent machine phases. The context object (VendingMachine) delegates all action calls to the current state object, which controls state changes based on logic rules.

title: Parking Lot System
summary: Design a multi-level parking lot that can accommodate different vehicle types, handle ticketing, and process billing dynamically.
requirements: Support multiple floors and spot types (Compact, Large, Motorcycle).
Process vehicle check-in (issue ticket, assign spot) and check-out (compute fee, release spot).
Thread-safe singleton control for the main ParkingLot instance.
Support dynamic fare calculation based on hour counts and vehicle types.
erDiagram:
ParkingLot [1] <---> [*] ParkingFloor
ParkingFloor [1] <---> [*] ParkingSpot
ParkingTicket [1] <---> [1] ParkingSpot
ParkingTicket [1] <---> [1] Vehicle
classes: ParkingSpot (Base class for CompactSpot, LargeSpot, etc.)
Vehicle (Base class for Car, Motorcycle, Truck)
ParkingTicket (Tracks entry/exit times and spot references)
approach: Define a clean class hierarchy for Vehicles and ParkingSpots. Ensure concurrency control on spot allocation (synchronized methods) so multiple gates don't double-book the same physical spot.

title: Snake & Ladder
summary: Design a modular Snake and Ladder board game supporting multiple players and custom board dimensions.
requirements: Board contains Snakes and Ladders at randomized positions.
Process turns iteratively using a dice (1-6 rolls).
Winning condition: Player lands exactly on slot 100.
erDiagram:
Game [1] <---> [1] Board
Game [1] <---> [*] Player
Board [1] <---> [100] BoardCell
BoardCell [1] <---> [0..1] Jump (Snake/Ladder)
classes: BoardCell (Contains optional Snake or Ladder pointer)
approach: Use a Queue to manage player turn sequences. Model snakes and ladders as jumping destinations on BoardCell nodes to keep board rendering and trajectory computation decoupled.

title: Library Management System
summary: Design a library cataloging system tracking book stock, borrowing status, user fines, and custom search filters.
requirements: Support books with multiple copies (each copy has a unique Barcode).
Search catalog by title, author, subject, or publication date.
Enforce maximum loan limits (e.g., 5 books for 14 days) and compute daily late fees.
erDiagram:
Library [1] <---> [*] BookCopy
BookCopy [1] <---> [0..1] BookLending
Member [1] <---> [*] BookLending
BookCopy [1] <---> [1] Book
approach: Implement search strategies using indexes on HashMaps to enable fast retrieval. Synchronize the lending action to handle concurrent checkout attempts of the last book copy.

title: Cricbuzz
summary: Design Cricbuzz/Cricinfo to display live cricket match scorecards, player statistics, and run notifications.
requirements: Provide real-time updates of overs, runs, wickets, and run rates.
Push scoreboard modifications immediately to observers (web dashboard, SMS notifier).
Compute statistics (Strike rate, economy, target runs) dynamically.
erDiagram:
Match [1] <---> [2] Innings
Match [1] <---> [*] Observer
Innings [1] <---> [*] PlayerStats
approach: Use the Observer pattern to decouple Match engine updates from dashboard rendering layouts. Compute statistics on the fly inside the Scorecard model.

title: Restaurant
summary: Design a Restaurant management system handling table reservations, dining orders, chef notifications, and bills.
requirements: Support dynamic table status mapping (Free, Reserved, Occupied).
Process orders containing multiple menu items and customize chef alerts.
Calculate checks with optional tax, service fee, and discount vouchers.
erDiagram:
Restaurant [1] <---> [*] Table
Table [1] <---> [0..1] Order
Order [1] <---> [*] MenuItem
Order [1] <---> [1] Bill
classes: Order (List of items, table reference, status)
approach: Table booking must be thread-safe to prevent double reservation. Use Observer to push active kitchen order updates directly to the chefs' display.

title: Cache (LRU / LFU)
summary: Design a thread-safe in-memory cache supporting eviction policies like Least Recently Used (LRU) or Least Frequently Used (LFU).
requirements: Constant time complexity O(1) for get and put operations.
Clean eviction trigger once the cache reaches max capacity limit.
Full thread-safety under concurrent reads and writes.
erDiagram:
LRUCache [1] <---> [*] Node
LRUCache [1] <---> [1] DoublyLinkedList
approach: Combine a HashMap (for O(1) lookups) with a custom Doubly Linked List (for O(1) updates/removals). Protect operations using locks or synchronization blocks.

title: File System
summary: Design an in-memory Directory and File management system that processes nesting structures and size computations.
requirements: Directories can contain files or other nested directories.
Calculate directory sizes dynamically (sum of all nested contents).
Support standard actions like list, delete, and search.
erDiagram:
Directory [1] <---> [*] FileSystemEntry
FileSystemEntry <|-- File
FileSystemEntry <|-- Directory
approach: Implement the Composite design pattern. Ensure directories and files inherit from the same base class to process sizes and listing routines uniformly.

title: Amazon Locker
summary: Design a package delivery locker system that matches packages to available lockers, manages security PINs, and handles returns.
requirements: Locker sizes must match package dimensions (Small, Medium, Large).
Generate a secure 6-digit PIN on package delivery for customer pickup.
Enforce pickup timeout limits (release locker if not claimed in 3 days).
erDiagram:
LockerService [1] <---> [*] Locker
Locker [1] <---> [0..1] Package
approach: Optimize matching using dynamic strategies (first fit, best fit). Use background cron processes to clean expired locks and flag unclaimed lockers.

title: Online Auction System
summary: Design an online auction platform that handles dynamic item listings, real-time bidding, and notifications.
requirements: Auctions transition from Created -> Active -> Completed -> Cancelled.
Bid validation: incoming bid must exceed current highest bid by minimum increment.
Broadcast updates to all active bidders when a higher bid is accepted.
erDiagram:
AuctionItem [1] <---> [*] Bid
AuctionItem [1] <---> [*] Observer (Bidders)
approach: Use synchronizations on bid actions to solve race conditions when bids arrive simultaneously in the final seconds of an auction.

title: BookMyShow (Movie Ticket Booking)
summary: Design a movie ticket booking platform handling massive concurrent seat selection, payments, and notifications.
requirements: Allow users to browse shows in specific theaters.
Ensure seat allocation is highly concurrent: lock seat temporarily (e.g., 5 mins) during payment checkout.
Release seat locks if checkout session expires or payment fails.
Support multiple payment gateways.
erDiagram:
Theater [1] <---> [*] Screen
Screen [1] <---> [*] Show
Show [1] <---> [*] Seat
Booking [1] <---> [*] Seat
Booking [1] <---> [1] User
classes: Seat (Gold, Silver, status: FREE, LOCKED, BOOKED)
approach: Implement optimistic or pessimistic locking on seats to prevent double booking. Use a Redis-based cache to store temporary seat locks with TTLs (Time-To-Live) corresponding to payment checkout timers.

title: Splitwise (Expense Sharing App)
summary: Design Splitwise to add expenses, share balances among friends, and optimize debt simplification.
requirements: Users can log group expenses split equally, unequally, or by percentages.
Automatically compute balances between users.
Provide a debt-simplification algorithm (minimize transactions between members).
erDiagram:
Group [1] <---> [*] User
Group [1] <---> [*] Expense
Expense [1] <---> [*] Split
User [1] <---> [1] BalanceSheet
approach: Compute net balance sheets for all users. Run a greedy optimization algorithm (using a Min Heap for debtors and a Max Heap for creditors) to iteratively settle the largest debts, minimizing the total number of transactions.

title: Elevator System
summary: Design a controller for a bank of elevators optimizing request handling, speed, and energy across multiple floors.
requirements: Elevators must go Up, Down, or stay Idle.
Dispatch algorithm should pick the optimal elevator for internal and external requests.
Handle state transitions cleanly (doors opening, closing, moving, emergency stops).
Support request queues with direction optimization (e.g. SCAN algorithm).
erDiagram:
ElevatorController [1] <---> [*] ElevatorCar
ElevatorCar [1] <---> [1] ElevatorState
ElevatorCar [1] <---> [*] Request
approach: Use the State pattern to decouple direction & floor transition logics from the main Elevator class. Maintain separate sorted sets (upQueue and downQueue) for SCAN-based request handling.

title: Chess
summary: Design a chess engine supporting pieces behavior, move validations, board states, checkmate rules, and move rollbacks.
requirements: White and Black players execute turn-based moves.
Pieces (King, Queen, Pawn) validate their legal trajectories.
Checkmate/stalemate validation algorithms.
Support transaction history/undo moves.
erDiagram:
Game [1] <---> [1] Board
Game [1] <---> [2] Player
Board [1] <---> [64] Cell
Cell [1] <---> [0..1] Piece
Game [1] <---> [*] Move
classes: Cell (Tracks Row, Column, and current Piece)
Move (Tracks start and end Cells, capturing history)
approach: Model Chess pieces by subclassing a base Piece class with custom canMove rules. Use the Command pattern for Move objects to support action validation, turn logs, and undo actions.

title: ATM Machine
summary: Design an ATM controller that processes cards, verifies security PINs, updates accounts, and dispenses cash.
requirements: ATM transitions: Idle -> HasCard -> PinEntered -> Dispensing -> TransactionComplete.
Support multiple currency denominations ($10, $20, $50, $100).
Implement ATM cash limits and roll back transactions if dispenser fails.
erDiagram:
ATM [1] <---> [1] ATMState
ATM [1] <---> [1] CashDispenser
CashDispenser [1] <---> [0..1] CashDispenser (Next Chain)
classes: CashDispenser (Base link handler in Chain of Responsibility)
approach: Combine the State pattern (for card handling steps) with Chain of Responsibility (for resolving cash dispensing counts of bills).

title: Hotel Management
summary: Design a hotel management dashboard managing rooms, dynamic pricing, service bookings, and checkouts.
requirements: Rooms categories: Single, Double, Suite, Deluxe.
Dynamic price adjustment based on occupancy, season, and room class.
Support reservation lifecycle: Booked, CheckedIn, CheckedOut, Cancelled.
erDiagram:
Hotel [1] <---> [*] Room
Room [1] <---> [*] Reservation
Reservation [1] <---> [1] Customer
Reservation [1] <---> [1] Bill
classes: Room (Room number, Category, Status: Free, Occupied, OutOfOrder)
approach: Separate Reservation entities from Room entities. Use state patterns for Room cleaning flows, and lock rooms during booking transactions.

title: Uber
summary: Design the class system for Uber/Lyft matching rider queries to optimal local drivers in real-time.
requirements: Riders input source and destination coordinates to request trip.
Find closest active driver within N miles (e.g. using spatial matching).
Dynamic trip pricing strategies (surge based on active trip demand/drivers count).
erDiagram:
Rider [1] <---> [*] Trip
Driver [1] <---> [*] Trip
Trip [1] <---> [1] Location (Start)
Trip [1] <---> [1] Location (End)
classes: Trip (Source, Destination, Driver, Rider, Fare, Status)
approach: Match drivers using spatial grid grids (like geohash/S2 cells). Decouple surge calculations into Strategy patterns for easy live testing overrides.

title: Google Docs
summary: Design a collaborative real-time document editor class system managing operational transforms and cursor syncing.
requirements: Support concurrent text edits from multiple user connections.
Resolve edit conflicts using Operational Transformation (OT) or Conflict-free Replicated Data Types (CRDT).
Broadcast updates to active participants dynamically.
erDiagram:
Document [1] <---> [*] TextOperation
Document [1] <---> [*] UserSession
classes: OTEngine (Transforms operations so they execute consistently)
UserSession (Tracks connection socket and cursor position)
approach: Maintain absolute index offsets on operations. Transform indices on concurrent inserts using standard client-server sequence IDs to maintain convergence.

title: Airline Reservation
summary: Design an airline reservation system processing flight schedules, aircraft seats allocation, and payment locks.
requirements: Manage airline inventories (flights, seats by class: Economy, Business).
Process seat reserves using pessimistic concurrency control.
Generate passenger flight schedules, boarding passes, and ticket updates.
erDiagram:
Flight [1] <---> [*] FlightInstance
FlightInstance [1] <---> [*] FlightSeat
Reservation [1] <---> [*] FlightSeat
Reservation [1] <---> [1] User
classes: FlightInstance (Specific departure date, seat status map)
approach: Use database row-level locking (SELECT ... FOR UPDATE) during seat selection to avoid overbooking the same physical aircraft seats.

title: WhatsApp / Chat System
summary: Design a chat application managing real-time connections, chat history, and dynamic user statuses.
requirements: Deliver messages in real-time when recipient is online.
Queue messages for offline users and deliver them once they connect.
Support read receipts (Sent -> Delivered -> Read status).
erDiagram:
User [1] <---> [*] UserConnection
User [1] <---> [*] ChatMessage
ChatMessage [1] <---> [1] MessageStatus
approach: Protect the active connections directory using concurrent collections. Use message queues to isolate offline delivery logic from active socket threads.

title: Inventory System
summary: Design a warehouse inventory system tracking stock levels, processing order picks, and alerting low stock events.
requirements: Track quantities of products across different warehouse layouts.
Reserve stock immediately during checkout.
Trigger notifications to suppliers once inventory falls below a threshold.
erDiagram:
Warehouse [1] <---> [*] InventoryItem
InventoryItem [1] <---> [1] StockLevel
Order [1] <---> [*] OrderItem
approach: Apply pessimistic locks to individual inventory SKU rows to prevent transaction race conditions during popular flash sales.

title: Ride Sharing
summary: Design a ride-sharing pool system (similar to Uber Pool) where riders share a trip and split fares dynamically.
requirements: Match riders travelling along similar routes (coordinate routes checks).
Calculate fare dynamically based on distance, time, and active splits.
Real-time coordination of driver pick-up sequences.
erDiagram:
PoolTrip [1] <---> [1..4] Rider
PoolTrip [1] <---> [1] Driver
PoolTrip [1] <---> [1] Route
Route [1] <---> [2..8] Location
classes: PoolTrip (Multiple Rider list, Driver, Route mapping)
Route (Sequence of pick-up and drop-off coordinates)
approach: Model routing as a directed graph traversal problem. Decouple pickup sequences from driver location tracking using a modular route manager.
